sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Judy Gomez, is a 61-year-old female with ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
She reports doing well, with a decrease in pain since the last visit, where the prednisone dose was decreased.
She mentions occasional increased pain in her feet due to her desk job, but this is well-controlled with occasional extra doses of prednisone.
She reports taking an extra dose of prednisone once a week, but this is not often.
She attributes her recent experience of feeling horrible after getting the COVID-19 vaccine to not taking methotrexate the week before the first shot.
She reports feeling better after taking the medication the following week.
She expresses concern about bruising, particularly after bumping herself, and did not know it was related to the prednisone.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of psoriatic arthritis, for which she is currently being treated with methotrexate and prednisone.
Review of systems: The patient reports bruising, particularly after bumping herself.
Current medications: I prescribed methotrexate for psoriatic arthritis.
I prescribed prednisone 1 tablet per day for psoriatic arthritis.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient had a good range of motion in her toes, with ridges and nail changes consistent with psoriasis.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has psoriatic arthritis, which is currently being well-managed with methotrexate and a decreased dose of prednisone.
Problem list: Psoriatic arthritis; confirmed; suspected bruising due to prednisone use.
Plan:
I will discontinue the patient's prednisone and continue her on methotrexate.
I will instruct her to schedule an appointment for blood work at the front desk.
I will see her in 3 months to follow up on her treatment.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in 3 months for a follow-up appointment.
